name.es: Sel Roti (pan de arroz nepalí en forma de anillo)
name.fr: Sel Roti (pain de riz népalais en anneau)

description.en: A ring-shaped Nepali bread piped by hand from a sweet rice-and-banana batter straight into hot oil. Crackling crisp outside and soft within, it is the signature treat of the Dashain and Tihar festivals across Nepal, Sikkim and Darjeeling.
description.de: Ein ringförmiges nepalesisches Brot, das von Hand aus einem süßen Reis-Bananen-Teig direkt in heißes Öl gespritzt wird. Außen knackig knusprig, innen weich, ist es die Signature-Leckerei der Dashain- und Tihar-Feste in Nepal, Sikkim und Darjeeling.
description.es: Un pan nepalí en forma de anillo, formado a mano con una masa dulce de arroz y plátano directamente en aceite caliente. Crujiente y crepitante por fuera, suave por dentro, es el dulce insignia de los festivales Dashain y Tihar en Nepal, Sikkim y Darjeeling.
description.fr: Un pain népalais en anneau, façonné à la main à partir d'une pâte sucrée de riz et de banane directement dans l'huile chaude. Croustillant dehors, moelleux dedans, douceur emblématique des festivals Dashain et Tihar au Népal, au Sikkim et à Darjeeling.

origin.note: Centered on the Dashain and Tihar festivals, when households fry large batches to share with neighbors and visiting family.

ingredients.note: short-grain rice, soaked 5-6 hours then ground to a paste
for soaking the rice and thinning the batter; source did not fully quantify, estimated
for deep-frying; amount absorbed is estimated

keeping.note: Best fresh, still faintly warm and crackly; toughens by the next day. Freezes reasonably and can be gently reheated.

method.title: Soak rice
method.summary: Rinse rice and soak 5-6 hours or overnight, then drain.

method.title: Blend
method.summary: Blend the soaked rice with milk and a little water to a thick, smooth paste.

method.title: Season
method.summary: Mix in sugar, mashed banana, cardamom, melted ghee and a pinch of salt until the batter is lump-free and pourable; rest covered at least 30 minutes.

method.title: Heat oil
method.summary: Heat oil in a deep pan to medium temperature, hot enough that a drop of batter sizzles and rises right away.

method.title: Pipe rings
method.summary: Pour or pipe the batter directly into the hot oil in a ring shape, by hand or with a piping bag.

method.title: Fry and drain
method.summary: Fry one or two rings at a time, turning gently, until golden brown on both sides, then drain on paper towels.

source.adaptation: Omitted the source's optional wheat-flour binder for a traditional rice-only batter; added estimated water since the source didn't specify how much thins the batter; fry-oil absorption estimated.
